content: Cow milk contains casein proteins, primarily A1 casein, which includes BCM7, linked to cancer, digestive issues, and mental disorders.
Goat and sheep milk contain A2 casein proteins, which are less likely to cause inflammation due to their genetic origin from 10,000 years ago.
Sheep yogurt made from sheep milk has virtually no lactose, making it suitable for lactose intolerant individuals, while sheep milk cheese is nearly lactose-free due to the culturing process.
Goat and sheep milk are more bioavailable sources of protein compared to cow milk, enhancing the absorption of vital minerals like phosphorus and magnesium.
Goat milk and sheep milk are recommended as alternatives to cow milk for those seeking a healthier, less inflammatory dairy option.
